sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard, which supersedes individual standards for canned grapefruit (CXS 15 -1981) and canned mandarin oranges (CXS 68 -1981), applies to certain canned citrus fruits, as defined in Section 2 below, and offered for direct consumption, including for catering purposes or for repacking if required. It does not apply to the product when indicated as being intended for further processing.

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text: Canned citrus fruit is the product: (a) prepared from washed, sound and mature ripe grapefruit (Citrus paradisi Macfadyen), mandarin oranges (Citrus reticulate Blanco, including all the suitable commercial varieties for canning), sweet orange varieties (Citrus sinensis (L.), Osbeck, including all the suitable commercial varieties for canning) or pummelo (Citrus Maxima Merr. or Citrus grandis [L.]); (b) packed with water or other suitable liquid packing medium, sugars as defined in the Standard for Sugars (CXS 212-1999),1 honey as defined in the Standard for Honey (CXS 12-1981),2 suitable spices or flavouring ingredients appropriate to the product; and (c) processed by heat, in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to prevent spoilage. Before processing, the fruit shall have been properly washed and peeled and the membrane, seeds and core and fibre strands originating from albedo or core, shall have been substantially removed from the sections.

sections.text: Product Wholei Broken Twin Pieces Canned grapefruit Not less than 75 % of original segment Less than 75 % of original segment  Canned sweet orange   Canned pummelo Not less than 50 % of original segment Less than 50 % of original segment Large enough to remain on a screen having 8 mm2 openings formed by a wire of 2 mm diameter Canned mandarin orange Not less than 75 % of original segment Not less than 50 % of original segment but large enough to remain on a screen having 8 mm2 openings formed by wire of 2 mm diameter. See definition for whole except two or three segments joined together, which have not been separated during processing. A segment which is split in one place only and is not prone to disintegrate shall be considered whole, but parts of a segment joined by a “thread”, or by membrane only shall not be considered “whole”.

sections.title: Other styles (canned grapefruit, mandarin oranges, sweet orange varieties and pummelos)
path_titles: Other styles (canned grapefruit, mandarin oranges, sweet orange varieties and pummelos)
sections.text:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:
(a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard;
(b) meets all relevant requirements of the standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements which are applicable to that style which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
(c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: Designation in accordance with size
sections.parent_title: Sizes in whole segment style (canned mandarin oranges only)
path_titles: Sizes in whole segment style (canned mandarin oranges only)
sections.text:
Canned mandarin oranges in whole segment style may be designated according to size in the following manner:
(a) Uniform single size
(i) “Large” – 20 or less whole segments per 100 g of drained fruit.
(ii) “Medium” – 21 to 35 whole segments per 100 g of drained fruit.
(iii) “Small” – 36 or more whole segments per 100 g of drained fruit.
(iv) Single sizes shall also meet the uniformity requirements of Section 3.2.5.
(b) Mixed sizes – A mixture of two or more single sizes.

sections.citation: 57aa887b-c886-4568-bc63-162de17b5d78, For canned grapefruit, canned sweet orange varieties and canned pummelo, p. 5
sections.text:
The finished product shall be prepared from such materials and under such practices that it shall be reasonably free from extraneous fruit matter such as peel or core or albedo and shall not contain excessive defects whether specifically mentioned in this standard or not. Certain common defects should not be present in amounts greater than the following limitations:
(i) The total surface covered by membrane shall not exceed 20 cm2 per 500 g of total contents.
(ii) Developed seeds shall not exceed four per each 500 g of total contents. A developed seed is defined as a seed which measures more than 9 mm in any dimension.
(iii) Not more than 15 percent by weight of the drained fruit may be blemished units. A blemished unit is a fruit section or any portion thereof which is damaged by lye peeling, by discolouration, or by any other visible injury.

sections.title: Minimum drained weight
sections.text: 7.1.4.1 The minimum drained weight shall be as follows: (a) For canned grapefruit, canned sweet orange varieties and and pummelos – the drained weight of the product shall be not less than 50 percent, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20 °C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled. (b) For canned pummelo – the drained weight of the product shall be not less than 40 percent, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20 °C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled. (c) For canned mandarin oranges – the drained weight of the product shall be not less than 56 percent, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20 °C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

(c) For canned mandarin oranges – the drained weight of the product shall be not less than 56 percent, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20 °C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

sentences.original: Canned citrus fruit is the product: (a) prepared from washed, sound and mature ripe grapefruit (Citrus paradisi Macfadyen), mandarin oranges (Citrus reticulate Blanco, including all the suitable commercial varieties for canning), sweet orange varieties (Citrus sinensis (L.), Osbeck, including all the suitable commercial varieties for canning) or pummelo (Citrus Maxima Merr. or Citrus grandis [L.]); (b) packed with water or other suitable liquid packing medium, sugars as defined in the Standard for Sugars (CXS 212-1999),1 honey as defined in the Standard for Honey (CXS 12-1981),2 suitable spices or flavouring ingredients appropriate to the product; and (c) processed by heat, in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to prevent spoilage.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

sentences.original:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:
(a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard;
(b) meets all relevant requirements of the standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements which are applicable to that style which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
(c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sentences.original:
Canned mandarin oranges in whole segment style may be designated according to size in the following manner:
(a) Uniform single size
(i) “Large” – 20 or less whole segments per 100 g of drained fruit.
(ii) “Medium” – 21 to 35 whole segments per 100 g of drained fruit.
(iii) “Small” – 36 or more whole segments per 100 g of drained fruit.
(iv) Single sizes shall also meet the uniformity requirements of Section 3.2.5.
(b) Mixed sizes – A mixture of two or more single sizes.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CERTAIN CANNED CITRUS FRUITS
pages.summary: Página apresenta um título principal em caixa alta sem numeração e um cabeçalho. Não há outras seções identificadas. Texto associado é uma breve descrição da adoção e emendas do documento.

pages.title: CXS 254-2007
pages.summary: A página apresenta um cabeçalho com título do documento e três seções principais em caixa alta, cada uma com texto explicativo direcionado a emendas e correções específicas. Não há continuidade de conteúdo entre páginas.

A página contém um cabeçalho com código e número de página. Identificam-se duas seções principais numeradas (1. SCOPE e 2. DESCRIPTION), com várias subseções numeradas dentro da seção 2, incluindo definições e estilos detalhados. A maior parte do texto está associada à subseção 2.1 e 2.3.1, outras subdivisões estão presentes mas com texto mínimo ou ausente na página. Não há continuidade de conteúdo entre páginas.

A página apresenta várias seções e subseções numeradas, começando na continuidade da seção 2 e iniciando a seção 3. Foi identificado que a seção 2.3.2 segue da página anterior, pois seu conteúdo continua. A seção 3 inicia nesta página e segue para a próxima. Textos são associados diretamente aos seus títulos numerados conforme o padrão. Cabeçalho da página é identificado como CXS 254-2007  4, sem título principal do documento nesta página.

A página apresenta a continuação da seção 3.2 com subseções 3.2.5 e 3.2.6. A seção 3 avança com as subseções 3.3 e 3.4, finalizando com o início da seção 4 como título em caixa alta sem numeração. Textos são associados diretamente aos seus títulos. O cabeçalho da página está identificado como CXS 254-2007  5. O texto sugere que a seção 3.2 já vinha da página anterior (continuidade), e as subseções são subdivididas em níveis hierárquicos coerentes para RAG.

A página 6 contém os títulos principais de nível 1: Seções 5 CONTAMINANTS, 6 HYGIENE e 7 WEIGHTS AND MEASURES. As seções 5 e 6 contêm textos explicativos diretamente associados. A seção 7 possui subtítulos aninhados, incluindo 7.1 Fill of container e suas subseções 7.1.1 a 7.1.4, com detalhamento até o nível 4 (7.1.4.2). Todos os textos estão associados aos seus respectivos títulos. Não há indicações de continuidade de conteúdo para outras páginas. O cabeçalho da página está identificado como CXS 254-2007  6.

A página contém as seções principais 8 LABELLING e 9 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING. A seção 8 é detalhada com subtítulos numerados até nível 4, porém a maioria do texto é indentada para seções específicas. O texto está organizado associadamente a cada subseção. Não há continuidade explícita de conteúdo vindo da página anterior ou continuando para a próxima. O cabeçalho da página é identificado claramente como CXS 254-2007  7.
A página contém um único nó estrutural de nível 1 intitulado 'NOTES' que agrega diversas referências apontadas numericamente. Não há subseções detectadas nem continuidade com outras páginas.
